tasks.title: RegimeResolver core: Target -> Regime -> Lane, config-not-code, one-regime, fail-closed ambiguity, shared object-model (TG-110)

scenarios: A target resolves to exactly one regime and its lane
An unknown regime falls back to the default lane or refuses and an ambiguous regime fails closed
Regime resolution matches on the same estate object-model as policy and credential

tasks.title: Lane abstraction + interceptor composition seam: an effect leaf reachable only through spec/013 Do, never a bypass (TG-110)

scenarios: Every lane is reachable only through the actuation interceptor chain

tasks.title: AWX-job actuator (the FIRST lane, the buildable slice): allowlisted+policy-authorized template, typed extra_vars, credential+mode compose, sensor vs mutating, native Go distroless (TG-110)

scenarios: The AWX-job actuator launches only an allowlisted policy-authorized template
The AWX-job effect is a template plus typed extra_vars and rejects unknown keys
The AWX-job lane resolves the token after policy and launches only while the mode chokepoint permits
A read-only setup job is a sensor and a mutating template stays off until the flip
The AWX token is a sealed SecretRef and the sensor token is read-only and distinct

tasks.title: Async-verify channel (GLOBAL): job handle + poll-to-terminal, launch-as-prediction verdict, pending-verification+timeout-unverified, idempotent action_id binding (TG-110)

scenarios: An async actuation returns a job handle and is polled to a terminal state
The launch is a prediction verified against the terminal outcome and fed to the graduation ladder
A launched but unverified job counts as no clean run and times out to unverified
A second launch for the same action_id is refused

tasks.title: Playbooks-as-knowledge lane: read-only AWX template+description+inventory ingest into wiki+RAG, re-read by id, launches nothing (TG-110)

scenarios: The knowledge lane ingests templates and inventory read-only re-read by id
The knowledge lane launches nothing and only proposes a runbook

tasks.title: regime_resolution + regime_actuation + deferred_verdict persistence migration + audit-every-resolution/launch/verdict, append-only, no secret (TG-110)

scenarios: Every resolution launch and verdict is appended to the ledger with no secret and the tables are append-only

tasks.title: Console regime UX (read-only): per-target regime/lane map, AWX template-allowlist with authorized op-class, pending-verification queue, lane-coverage view from real /v1/regime state (TG-110)

scenarios: The console renders the regime map allowlist pending-verification queue and lane coverage
Every allowlist edit is appended to the tamper-evident ledger

tasks.note: Read-only slice DONE: the regime panel (frontend/src/panels/regime) renders REAL engine state from GET /v1/regime — the per-target regime→lane map, lane-coverage (wired vs inert/fail-closed), the observed AWX template-allowlist (template→op-class), the pending-verification queue, and the actuation tail — self-fetched via the shared query client, fail-closed on error, honest empty state at Shadow. It's registered in the app nav (app.tsx) and reuses the existing panel/guarded-state/a11y visual system. The console React oracles are in regime.test.tsx; the Go acceptance read scenario (@REQ-1716, present) binds the /v1/regime wire contract the panel depends on. FOLLOW-ON (still @pending, scenario 'Every allowlist edit is appended to the tamper-evident ledger'): the ledger-audited allowlist EDITOR is a mutation-adjacent write path needing an authenticated write API + AuthAdminSession + audit; scoped OUT of this read-only MR.

tasks.title: Durable pgx pending-verification store: a launched job's deferred verify SURVIVES a worker restart (flip-prereq); UNIQUE action_id idempotency atomic at the DB; INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE but NOT DELETE (TG-110)

scenarios: A second launch for the same action_id is refused
A launched but unverified job counts as no clean run and times out to unverified

tasks.note: Durability slice DONE: the async-verify channel's pending-verification record (regime.PendingStore) is now backed by the DURABLE pgx table pending_verification (migration 0022) instead of the in-memory fake, so a launched AWX job's deferred verify SURVIVES a worker restart — a flip-prerequisite (before this, a restart between a launch and its terminal poll would forget the launch and lose the launch-as-prediction discipline). This record is MUTABLE (unlike the append-only 0020 audit tables T-017-6 built): a launch is reserved (state='pending'), a handle is bound, then it transitions EXACTLY ONCE to terminal 'verified' or bound-elapsed 'unverified'. So the runtime role KEEPS INSERT/SELECT/UPDATE but LOSES DELETE — action_id is the PRIMARY KEY (the idempotency guard, REQ-1712: a 2nd Reserve raises a 23505 unique-violation the pgx store maps to ErrDuplicatePending → ErrDuplicateLaunch, atomic at the DB, never a read-then-write race), and a claimed id is retained forever so a retry/re-poll/redelivery can never double-launch and a resolved record stays auditable/visible (REQ-1711). Single-org (no tenant_id), NON-SECRET (the AWX token is a SecretRef held elsewhere; only the non-secret job_id + the NUL-free committed prediction jsonb are stored), parameterized SQL only, schema_version reader-guard. core/db/regime_pending_write.go implements the interface EXACTLY (compile-time assertion) and the in-memory MemPendingStore stays the CI oracle; a shared store-contract test runs the SAME assertions against BOTH (mem in CI + pgx DSN-gated) and a pure-Go prediction round-trip test guards the 'pgx-fake-hides-a-dropped-field' class. Wired in cmd/worker/main.go (durable when the pool exists; mem fallback keeps the channel wired with no DB). core/regime/* is UNCHANGED (the interface + channel logic were already done in T-017-4). Mutation stays OFF: this is verify-state persistence — it launches nothing and actuates nothing (the table is empty at Shadow).
